keyPointsSummary: Let's = Let us (short form). Formula: Let's + base verb. Used for suggestions that include the speaker.
Negative form: Let's not + base verb. NEVER say 'Let's don't' — that is incorrect.
Formal version: Let us + base verb. Used in speeches, formal writing, official notices.
Always use base verb after Let's — no 'to', no '-ing', no third-person 's'.
'Let's' (with apostrophe) = Let us. 'Lets' (without apostrophe) = allows/permits — completely different meaning!
Contrast with Day 13: 'Let him/her/them' = permission to a 3rd person. 'Let's' = suggestion including speaker.
Motivational usage: 'Let's give it our best shot!', 'Let's make a fresh start.'
'Shall we?' can be used as a question form of Let's: 'Shall we begin?' = 'Let's begin?'

quickQuiz.hindi: चलो समय बर्बाद मत करते हैं।

quickQuiz.answer: Let us build a better tomorrow.

quickQuiz.hindi: चलो इस बारे में बहस मत करते हैं।
चलो एक नई शुरुआत करते हैं।
आइए सब मिलकर इस चुनौती का सामना करें।
quickQuiz.answer: Let us all face this challenge together.

quickQuiz.hindi: चलो deadline से पहले काम खत्म करते हैं।
quickQuiz.answer: Let's finish the work before the deadline.

quickQuiz.hindi: चलो अपनी गलतियों से सीखते हैं।
चलो किसी को परेशान मत करते हैं।
चलो साथ में celebrate करते हैं।
आइए इस अवसर का सदुपयोग करें।
quickQuiz.answer: Let us make the most of this opportunity.

quickQuiz.hindi: चलो एक deadline तय करते हैं।

mustRememberRules: Let's + base verb (NEVER Let's + to + verb or Let's + verb-ing)
Negative: Let's not + base verb (NEVER Let's don't + verb)
Formal: Let us + base verb (speeches, writing, official communication)
'Let's' (apostrophe) = Let us | 'Lets' (no apostrophe) = allows (e.g. He lets me go)
Let's = speaker included | Let him/her/them = permission to someone else (Day 13 contrast)
Question equivalent: 'Shall we + base verb?' = 'Let's + base verb?'
